name: Song Jiang
description: Song Jiang, also known as Song Gongming, is a pivotal figure in Chinese folklore and literature, most notably as the central character in the classic novel *Water Margin* (Shui Hu Zhuan). While not a deity in the traditional sense of being a naturally occurring divine being, Song Jiang's elevation to a near-divine status stems from his embodiment of justice, loyalty, and righteousness, virtues highly valued in Chinese culture. He is often venerated as a leader of righteous rebels who fought against corruption and tyranny during the Song Dynasty (though the historical basis for his existence is debated). His appeal lies in his ability to inspire loyalty and his strategic acumen, making him a complex figure whose actions are often morally ambiguous, yet ultimately aimed at a greater good. He represents a form of earthly heroism that transcends the mortal realm, earning him a place in the pantheon of folk heroes and figures worthy of veneration.
subtitle: Timely Rain

keyMyths.title: Song Jiang's Rebellion
keyMyths.description: Song Jiang's initial act of rebellion stems from accidentally killing his mistress and fleeing to Liangshan Marsh. This act, though born of misfortune, sets him on a path to becoming a leader of outlaws. He is constantly torn between his desire for imperial amnesty and his commitment to his brothers-in-arms, embodying the complexities of rebellion against established authority. This myth highlights his capacity for both violence and compassion.

keyMyths.title: Receiving the Heavenly Books
keyMyths.description: A crucial event in Song Jiang's legend involves receiving heavenly books (tian shu) from a mysterious Daoist immortal. These books contain strategic knowledge and prophecy, empowering him to lead the Liangshan outlaws with unparalleled success. This episode elevates Song Jiang beyond a mere mortal leader, imbuing him with a sense of divine purpose and legitimizing his rebellion against the corrupt imperial court. It also connects him to Daoist beliefs and cosmic order.

keyMyths.title: Accepting Imperial Amnesty
keyMyths.description: Despite his initial defiance, Song Jiang eventually accepts imperial amnesty and leads his Liangshan forces in campaigns against other rebel groups. This decision, though controversial, reflects his ultimate loyalty to the Song Dynasty and his desire to serve the people. His tragic end, poisoned by corrupt officials, solidifies his status as a tragic hero, forever bound to the fate of the dynasty he served.
